© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
When Grant first got started he spent 20 years knocking on doors to build a loyal
base of customers. He put more than 3 million miles of wear and tear on his body,
traveling across the country 250 days a year trying to get people to know him…
one door at a time.
Now because of social media, and most specifically because of Meta Ads (formally
Facebook Ads), everything has changed. Anybody who is willing to learn a skill and
invest a little money can reach more people in one week than he was able to talk to
over that entire 20 year period.
As you may know, Meta ads incorporate Facebook, Instagram and other Meta-owned
properties. Regardless of how you feel about the company or their networks, Meta ads
allow you to connect with the masses unlike any other platform. Meta ads are fast,
targeted, and effective.
All that said, Meta ads are not easy. Anytime you put your money on the line it is a
good idea to truly understand how it works and what it takes to succeed. While you’re
always going to have a learning curve, we want to reduce your frustration by teaching
you the “ins and outs”. This manual will teach you Meta ads the 10X way.
3
INTRODUCTION TO META ADS
This is the past 12 months on only one of our ad accounts
META ADS ARE OUR #1 SOURCE OF LEADS AND SALES (BY FAR)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 57 --

In addition to the basics, we’ve hand-picked many of our proven Meta ad strategies to
help you maximize the impact of every dollar spent. We’ve also provided video
tutorials, flowcharts, checklists, resources, and a step-by-step action manual.
Let’s get to it!
While it’s good to understand strategy, the best way to
learn Meta ads is to jump in. It will take a bit to get the
hang of it, but you can refer to your training as needed.

META MARKETING
TERMINOLOGY
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Here is a quick reference guide of terms and phrases you will come across while
advertising on Meta. Feel free to skim over this and come back to it as needed.
Remember that we live in a mobile world. 80% of our ads
are viewed on mobile, so take this into consideration with
your ad design, targeting, and every other aspect.
5
Showing your audiences two different versions of an ad and comparing the results to
determine a winner. Ad variations can include alternate images, headlines, body copy,
destination link, and more. Once the winner is chosen, the other ad is discontinued and
all future audiences would see the winning ad (until a new ad variant is tested.)
A/B TESTING
This is what a user on any Meta platform will see when they view your campaign on their
desktop, tablet, or mobile device. Ads have many different placements across the
Facebook & Instagram audience network. Ads include media (image or video), primary
text, headline, and a call to action button.
AD
When your Meta Ad competes in an auction with other similar ads. Meta measures the ad
quality, bid, and estimated action rates to decide which of the ads in the auction to show
to Facebook users at what time.
AD AUCTION
It’s a unique number given to all ads you’ve created.
AD ID
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Area of the Ads Manager where users manage the settings for budget, scheduling,
audience/targeting, placement, and delivery optimization.
AD SET
When someone (who came to your website from your Meta pixel ad) adds an item to the
cart — it shows up as an Add to Cart event in the reporting section. To see this in your ad
reports, add Facebook Pixel to your website.
ADD TO CART
Is a tool you can use to create, manage, and run ads on Meta as well as handle all your
payment and billing information related to advertising on Facebook. Ads Manager also
has an integrated analytical dashboard where you can measure the performance of your
ads.
ADS MANAGER
The users who will see your ads on any of the Meta platform properties.
AUDIENCE
A cluster of Facebook’s partners. It’s a wide range of mobile app and web publishers who
show your ads on their apps and websites.
AUDIENCE NETWORK
Average Cost-Per-Click is a number that shows how much each click your ad received
costs you on average (note that you don’t pay for the click, this is a reporting metric).
AVERAGE CPC
The amount you’re willing to pay for your ad to be shown to your audience. Bidding is
managed in the Ad Set area of the Ads Manager.
BID
Bid strategy defines a strategy that Meta uses to bid for you in the ad auction. When you
set the bid manually, choose from these strategy types: lowest cost, bid cap, cost cap,
highest value, target cost, or min ROAS.
BID STRATEGIES
It’s a field in a reporting table that tells you how much money you spent on an ad,
ad set, or campaign.
AMOUNT SPENT
6
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 57 --

Sponsored post on your Facebook or Instagram. By boosting a post on your Facebook
page, you can make it visible to Facebook users, who have not liked your page.
BOOSTED POST
Grouping your users by interests, likes, apps installed, and so on.
BROAD CATEGORIES
The amount you plan to spend on an ad campaign.
BUDGET
The cost to acquire a paying customer.
CAC (CUSTOMER ACQUISITION COST)
A campaign is the top-level category where you decide your ad objective, which is the
action you want people to take when they see your ads. Facebook has three objective
categories: Awareness, Consideration, and Conversion.
CAMPAIGN
When Meta automatically applies your campaign budget across your ad sets for you to
achieve the best campaign results. It is based on the potential of each ad set to attract the
best audience.
CAMPAIGN BUDGET OPTIMIZATION
Total amount of people who saw your ads in the campaign.
CAMPAIGN REACH
Maximum spending limit for each campaign. After the limit is reached, your campaign
stops running.
CAMPAIGN SPENDING LIMIT
7
Boosting a post is easy to do, but doesn’t give you
much control. Instead of boosting, you can create an
ad from the post inside the Ad Manager.
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
If your goal is to make a sale, chances are there are
several steps to get there. We’ll teach you how to track
each step to optimize ad performance.
When a person in your Facebook audience clicks on your ad to follow your call to action.
CLICKS
The people who liked your page that you want to include or exclude when choosing an
audience to target with your ad. Those people are grouped as Connections.
CONNECTIONS
The number of people to complete an action that has been pre-defined as a successful
goal or conversion. Goals can be webinar registrations, downloads, product purchases,
and more.
CONVERSIONS
The cost you’re paying for each successful campaign conversion.
COST PER CONVERSION
The cost to acquire a non-paying subscriber or a lead. Also known as CPL (cost per lead).
CPA (COST PER ACQUISITION)
The cost you are paying for each click on your ads.
CPC (COST PER CLICK)
The cost to acquire a non-paying subscriber or a lead. Also known as CPA (cost per
acquisition).
CPL (COST PER LEAD)
The average cost for 1,000 impressions.
CPM (COST PER THOUSAND IMPRESSIONS)
8
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 57 --

While the goal of your advertising is to make money,
realize the additional benefit of exposure. Even if you
break even, you are still getting known.
The number of times your ad was clicked on (clicks), divided by the total number of times
your ad was shown (impressions).
CTR (CLICK-THROUGH-RATE)
It is a targeting option you can choose when creating a Meta ad. To create a custom
audience you need to import details about your customers, such as email addresses or
actions made on your website.
CUSTOM AUDIENCE
It is the amount of money you want to spend on an ad set or campaign per day. Each ad
set can have its own daily budget.
DAILY BUDGET
It is the status of your campaigns, ad sets, and ads. The status is shown in the Campaigns
page of Meta Ads Manager and can be anything from active to paused, or off.
DELIVERY
It is the location you want people to land on after clicking on your ad or a CTA
(call-to-action) button. It can be your business’s website, messenger, Facebook page,
or an app.
DESTINATION
It’s a date your ad set is scheduled to end. You set it when creating the campaign.
END DATE
The number of people who responded Interested or Going to your Facebook event,
attributed to your ads.
EVENT RESPONSES
9
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 57 --

Word of caution: Meta uses the term “click” to
describe any click, even “See more” (to read your ad
text). Always refer to Link or Outbound Clicks.
This is the metric that defines the average number of times your ad was shown to the
same person.
FREQUENCY
The total number of times your ad was shown to a user on any Meta property.
IMPRESSIONS
It can be anything from clicking your ad to watching a video. For example: 1 click and
1 comment by 1 person is marked as 2 interactions.
INTERACTION
It’s an objective you can choose when creating your ads on Meta. When you set your
campaign objective as Lead Generation, you can ask your audience to fill out a form with
their contact details.
LEAD GENERATION
The amount you’ve set to be spent over the lifetime of the campaign.
LIFETIME BUDGET
It’s a way to target Facebook users based on the pages they like and the interests they
express on Facebook or Instagram.
LIKES & INTERESTS TARGETING
The number of clicks on links in the ad that lead to a destination on or off a Meta platform.
LINK CLICKS
Meta creates lookalike audiences similar to the ones that visit their website. To create a
lookalike audience, you need to have a Custom Audience set up first.
LOOKALIKE AUDIENCE
It indicates what you want to achieve with a campaign. It can be brand awareness or
reach, app installs, video views, catalog sales, and so on.
OBJECTIVE
10
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 57 --

The number of clicks on links that take people off Meta-owned properties like websites,
your app, or app deep links.
OUTBOUND CLICKS
A number of actions that Meta users performed on your business.
PAGE
Page or a post, due to your Facebook advertising efforts.
ENGAGEMENT
Meta Pixel is a code snippet you can insert into your website’s code. it helps Meta track all
events related to your ads that take place off Facebook.
PIXEL
Where you want your ads to be shown like News Feed, Suggested Video, Market Place,
Right Side Column, and so on. You can also let Facebook decide where to show your ads
with the automated placements option enabled.
PLACEMENT
The number of actions (likes, comments, shares, etc.) people performed on your page
post after they saw your boosted post or ad.
POST ENGAGEMENT
An estimate of how many people are in your chosen target audience.
POTENTIAL REACH
The total number of unique people who saw your ad.
REACH
A rating from 1 to 10 that estimates how well your target audience is responding to
your ad.
RELEVANCE SCORE
Marketing to a person who has indicated interest in your products and services.
Subjects of a retargeting campaign may have visited a product page on your
website, filled out a webform, or abandoned their cart.
RETARGETING (OR REMARKETING)
11
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 57 --

Retargeting ads on Meta are probably the most
cost-effective advertising in the world. The good news
is it is not that hard to do, and we’ll show you how.
A measure of the revenue your campaign generated, divided by the total amount
spent on ads.
ROAS (RETURN ON AD SPEND)
It is the audience a Lookalike Audience is based on.
SOURCE AUDIENCE
The number of times your video was played to completion, or for at least 15 seconds.
THRUPLAYS
Traffic is an objective you can choose when creating your ads. This objective focuses on
attracting as many people to your website as possible.
TRAFFIC
The number of times unique people clicked on your ads.
UNIQUE CLICKS
It is Unique clicks divided by unique impressions.
UNIQUE CTR
The number of times your ad was shown to a unique person.
UNIQUE IMPRESSIONS

Before getting started with Meta Ads, you’ll need to set up and connect a few things
within the Meta “ecosystem”. There are a few ways this can be set up, but this is the
way we recommend configuring these pieces…
It is worth noting that there are a few additional pieces that could be
added. For example, WhatsApp is a very large Meta property, but for
sake of simplicity, we’ll focus on the components above, and how they
interact with one another.
13
GETTING STARTED WITH
META ADS
META ECOSYSTEM AND SETUP
Here is a very quick explanation of each element and how they fit into the big picture…
Facebook Profile
This is your personal user account which is always under your name. You cannot do
anything on Facebook without a profile, and everything you do is connected to it.
Facebook Page
This is the specific page that you will need for your company (or a company you
represent) to do business on Facebook. Your ads will be attached to your page.
Instagram Account
While Instagram is not required, it is a massive advertising source and highly
recommended. You can easily turn any Instagram account into a business account in
the app settings, allowing you to run ads and attach your Instagram to your Meta
Business Manager.
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Facebook Profile
Facebook Page
Instagram Account
Meta Business Account
Meta Ad Account
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 57 --

If this sounds daunting, I understand. I hate this stuff too.
The good news is that once your accounts are set up you
can focus 100% of your energy on leads and sales.
Business Manager
This is the highest level of account in your Meta world. The business manager allows you
to manage more than one page and more than one ad account. It also allows you to
control who you let access your pages, ad accounts, and more.
Ad Manager
This is where you will create and manage all of your advertisements. We will spend most
of our efforts discussing how to master the ad manager.
The recommended order of operation is as follows (skip any steps already completed)…
NOTE: We provide additional details on the steps involved in the Facebook Account
Setup checklist.
14
Create a Facebook Profile (you likely already have one)
Create a Facebook Page (you can have multiple pages)
Create an Instagram Account (and turn into a business account)
Create a Meta Business Account (and connect your Page and Instagram)
Create a Meta Ad Account (from your business account)

As with anything, there are a small number of things to
focus on to get the most results. We’ll teach you what
matters most: taking the straightest path to making money.
While you can run an ad account without the business manager, it is highly
recommended that you make the Business Manager your central hub for all things
Meta...
Whenever you go to Facebook to do business, start at 10Xresource.com/meta
In addition to convenience and features, running all of our Meta activity out of your
Business Manager also allows you advantages as you grow and scale. For example,
audiences and pixel data from one ad account can be easily utilized in another account
as well (we’ll explain more about what that means in just a little bit!)
Additional details on setup are found in the provided video tutorial as well as in the set
up checklist. It is worth noting that you won’t need to worry about many of the available
features.

-- 16 of 57 --

A look inside one of our ad manager accounts. Notice you are viewing the Campaign tab.
The Ads Manager is where you will create and manage your ads. You can create a new ad
account from right inside your Business Manager.
While the Ads Manager can seem a little daunting at first, once you understand the
general navigation and get some practice, it is really quite manageable. We will discuss
the most important features and functionalities, and more importantly, ensure you know
what is needed to produce results.
The most important thing to understand about Meta ads is that the management is
broken into 3 levels. Here they are and the most important aspects of each level…
* Your budget can also be managed on the Ad Set level, but we usually use
Campaign Budget Optimization (CBO) because it is Meta’s preferred method and it
usually performs better.
16
CAMPAIGNS
Highest Level
Select Objective (goal of ads)
Budget* (usually daily spend)
ADS
Lowest Level
Ad Creative (image or video)
Ad Headline, Ad Copy, etc
AD SET
Middle Level
Target Audience (lots of options)
Placement (FB, Instagram, etc)
META ADS MANAGER

-- 17 of 57 --

To help wrap your head around this, you should go create a
test ad, from beginning to end. As long as you don’t
publish it in the last step, it won’t get sent to Meta.
Note that there are many more features and functionalities at every level, and we will go
into those in greater detail in the video tutorials and checklists. The important thing,
for now, is to understand the main concepts. It also helps to remember that...
You are able to set up an unlimited number of Campaigns, Ad sets, and Ads. Here is a
visual demonstration to help understand how these 3 levels work on a hierarchical level…
As you can see, an individual Ad can actually be used within more than one Campaign
or Ad Set. This is a point that many advertisers miss, and we’ll discuss why this is
important later.
Now that you conceptually understand the way the Ad Manager works, let’s take a
quick look at some of the functionality…
17
META AD SETUP IS A STEP-BY-STEP PROCESS
THAT TAKES YOU THROUGH ALL 3 LEVELS
Ad A	Ad A 	Ad B 	Ad B	Ad B 	Ad A 	Ad B 	Ad C	Ad A 	Ad B
Campaign 1 	Campaign 2 	Campaign 3
Adset 1.1 	Adset 1.2 	Adset 2.2	Adset 2.1 	Adset 2.3 	Adset 3.1

18
1 	Navigation Tabs
These allow you to go back and forth between each level (Campaigns, Ad Sets and Ads).
Remember that every ad you run will have settings and controls on all 3 levels.
Create Button
Click this button to create a new Campaign, Ad Set, or Ad (depending on which level
you are on)
Check Boxes
Checking these boxes for the appropriate row(s) allows you to perform any of the
desired actions such as duplicate, trash, edit, etc. This is the same functionality for
Campaigns, Ad Sets and Ads.
On/Off Switches
Toggle this switch to easily turn a Campaign, Ad Set, or Ad on or off (without deleting
any of the contents).
Duplicate
This can be used to make an exact copy at any level, making it easy to duplicate Campaigns,
Ad Sets and Ads.
Edit
This will allow you to go into the selected Campaign, Ad Set or Ad and edit settings.
Functionality will vary depending on which level you are on.
Trash
Use this to delete a Campaign, Ad Set, or Ad.

-- 19 of 57 --

If you’re like me you don’t like to waste time sorting through
worthless data. As soon as you get your first ad running,
customize your column view to see what matters most.
The last topic we’ll cover before jumping into ad creation is the importance of
configuring your Ad Manager. While Meta does provide valuable data in the default view,
it can make finding some important information a little too difficult (including how
much money you’ve spent!) The good news is that the columns displayed within each of
the 3 levels are easily customizable, and they only need to be edited once.
If any of these terms are unfamiliar they can all be found in the terminology section.
They are also explained in the step-by-step video tutorial which shows you exactly how
to do this.
8
9
10
Column Settings
This is where you can customize your view in the Ad Manager by selecting which data you
see and in what order. This is highly important and will be covered in great detail.
Breakdown
Use this to easily drill down and see more detail on your ad activity including factors like time,
viewer demographics, and the specific actions they took.
Reports
You will find a list of standard reports, or you can customize reports and save them for easy
reference later. Reports can also be exported from this section.

CAMPAIGN OBJECTIVES
Here’s a quick rundown of the various Campaign Objectives and what they do...
AD CAMPAIGNS
21
SETTING UP CAMPAIGNS AND ADS
The first step of the ad creation process is the Campaign level of the Ads Manager.
While there are several settings in this step, none is more
important than selecting the appropriate Objective.
The Objective you choose tells Meta what desired outcome
you want them to optimize for. The job of Meta’s
automated logic (the “algorithm”) is to serve your ads to
the members of your audience who are most likely to
complete your desired Objective.
Objective options available in Campaign Setup
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
The more data Meta has, the better they optimize your
ads. If you sell at a high price point or if you have a small
budget, a purchase objective may not provide sufficient
conversion data. Consider starting with a smaller
objective such as lead conversions.
Brand Awareness - Your ads will be shown to people who are most likely to
remember them.
Reach - Your ads will be shown to the maximum number of people.
Traffic - Your ads will be sent to people most likely to click and visit your website
Engagement - Your ads will be shown to people who are most likely to give Page
likes, comments, shares, or post reactions.
App Installs - Your ad will be shown to people who are most likely to download your
app.
Video Views - Your ad will be shown to people who are most likely to watch your
video.
Lead Generation - Actions are what Meta refers to as lead collection forms and are
shown to those most likely to sign up.
Messages - These ads use Facebook Messenger and are shown to people most likely
to send a message.
Conversions - Show your ads to people most likely to either sign up or purchase from
you (you can decide on your desired conversion in the Ad Set step).
Catalog Sales - These ads use Facebook’s catalog eCommerce feature and show to
people most likely to purchase.
Store Traffic - Your ad will be shown to people who are most likely to visit your
physical stores when nearby.
22
The most common type of campaign Objective to run is Conversions,
primarily because the end goal is usually to sell something. The
Conversion Objective also allows for a lot of flexibility. As you will
learn, you are able to optimize for any desired action on your
websites and funnels (these actions are what Facebook refers to as
an “Event”). We will cover this in full detail in the coming sections.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
With platforms like Facebook, remember that you’re
playing in their sandbox. If they recommend a feature,
it usually means they will favor advertisers who use it.
CAMPAIGN BUDGET OPTIMIZATION
In most situations, you’ll want to use a daily budget and the default setting for
campaign bid strategy. For example, you can use ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) as your
bidding strategy, but it will require you to set up a few tracking items which you might
not have in place at first.
Regarding your starting budget, you’ll want to be conservative at first, but also give
your ads a chance to succeed. For example, if you sell a $1000 product, you likely won’t
see much traction spending $10 a day. Set the initial budget at an amount you could
handle losing, but enough to give an indication of whether the campaign could work.
We usually start new campaigns at $500 a day, but $50 a day is a good starting point if
money is tight.
All that said, there is a time and a place to properly leverage every one of the
Objectives. As we’ll discuss further, your Objective will often be either based on the
audience you are targeting or a larger overall strategy.
Campaign Budget Optimization (CBO) allows you to manage your spending at the
Campaign level. We recommend turning this feature on, and so does Meta It is
especially helpful when you are new to advertising. The alternative, if CBO is not
activated, is to manage your spending on the Ad Set level.
23
When in doubt, you can always use the default settings and simply read the details!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 57 --

Meta does all ads are really just a big auction. There is a
limited amount of ad inventory, and whoever is willing to
pay the most gets it (and FB does all the bidding for you).
ADDITIONAL CAMPAIGN SETTINGS
Buying Type
You can choose to pay based on reach and frequency, but you’ll likely want to keep
the default of Auction.
Special Ad Category
You will only add something here if you advertise for credit offers, employment,
housing, or politics. If you do, you must claim your category and be sure to abide by
special rules.
A/B Test
This is where you can test Campaigns, Ad Sets, and Ads against each other. This is a
more advanced feature and will be set up after the ad is approved. You most likely
don’t need to do this as Meta is always optimizing these items anyway.
While these settings are generally left as the default value, here is a quick rundown on
what each is all about...
24

-- 25 of 57 --

AD SET LEVEL AND TARGETING
CONVERSION
So while we will go through all the Ad Set features in the
general order they will appear, we’ll spend the most
time on Audiences. Also keep in mind, while we will
cover basic audiences and targeting in this section, we
will continue to dig deeper into audiences throughout
this manual. Continue reading to learn more about
custom audiences, lookalike audiences, and retargeting.
Let’s get to it...
If you chose Conversion in the Campaign step, then the first thing Meta will ask in the
Ad Set step is what type of conversion you want to optimize for. If you choose another
objective type (such as Traffic or Reach) you will not see this step.
25
Your audience is half the battle with advertising
(the other half is your actual ads)
You will almost always choose Website, which will require some set up
What is a pixel? A pixel is a tracking code you install on your website or funnel
that allows information to be sent back to Meta. This is how Meta knows if you
generate a lead, make a sale, etc. We will cover this in greater detail, including
how to install, in the pixel and event section.
What is a conversion event? A conversion event is an action taken by a user
on your website that is reported back to Meta by your pixel. This will give
Facebook the specifics on what type of action was taken (whether it is a “lead”,
an “add to cart”, a “purchase”, etc) Again, we will cover this in more detail.
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 57 --

26
Don’t let technical jargon intimidate you. It isn’t as tough
as you might think, and once you wrap your head around
this stuff, you’ll have a huge competitive advantage.
Meta is going to prioritize advertisers who they know will
spend a lot of money forever. For that reason, don’t choose
an end date unless you have a reason to stop.
Dynamic Creative is a feature that makes it easy to test multiple variations of ads.
If you turn on Dynamic Creative you will be given the opportunity to add multiple
versions of various ad elements in the Ad step. For example, you can provide more than
one images, headlines, and ad copy. We will discuss testing in greater detail a little later.
If you’re just getting started, this may be a feature you want to leave off.
You can easily choose a start and end date for your campaigns. It is worth noting that
after you submit your ads, it usually takes a day or so to get them approved.
DYNAMIC CREATIVE
BUDGET AND SCHEDULE
Meta created this feature to help automate testing
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
27
Don’t be afraid to get creative and try new things with your
audiences. If you’re using CBO Meta will automatically shift
your budget to the audiences that work best.
Regarding budget, the functionality of budget on the Ad Set level will depend on
whether or not you turned on Campaign Budget Optimization (CBO) in the Campaign
step. If you did choose CBO, then the only budget settings you’ll see are the ability to put
spending limits (maximum and minimum) on your Ad Set.
So what type of targeting can you use when first starting out with Meta ads? While you
can’t start creating Custom Audiences or Lookalike Audiences until you’ve had some
activity (people landing on your website, liking your Facebook page, clicking your ads,
etc) the good news is that you do have options.
In addition to the basic targeting options such as location, age,
gender, and language, there are virtually limitless options when you
get into Detailed Targeting. Here are a few examples of Detailed
Targeting categories...
This area of Ads Manager empowers you with the ability to find prospects on Meta
platforms who you believe are most likely to respond favorably to your message. The first
thing to understand is the different types of Audiences that can be created on Meta...
AUDIENCES
You can combine any of these audience types
and you can also save any audience.
Basic/Detailed Targeting
These are all of the standard audience options that you are given in the Ad Set manager
without having any previous advertising history or pixel data.
Custom Audiences
These are audiences that you create based on user behavior. You create these audiences
from pixel data (Events such as purchases) or from activity on Facebook or Instagram.
Lookalike Audiences
These are audiences that you create to include people who are similar to one of your
Custom Audiences. For example, an audience that is similar to previous buyers.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

While everything organized is in categories, you can also simply start typing to get
recommendations…
Once you’ve learned how to add demographics, interests, and behaviors, you can start to
build your Audiences. Remember, Audiences can be any combination of these targeting
factors, as well as Custom Audiences (based on user activity) and/or Lookalike Audiences
(which are derivatives of your Custom Audiences). There is no limit to what you can add or
take away from your Audiences.

29
While targeting is important, the more narrow your
audience, the more you’ll pay to reach them. For cold ads,
try to keep your audience size at least two million.
Also remember that you can create as many Audiences as you want, and you can also
save your Audiences to use in other Ad Sets. Continually experiment until you find
winning combinations. Creating winning Audiences is almost as important as creating
winning ads. In fact, when you find a great audience it can often pay off for a lot longer
than a winning ad will.
In addition to WHO you want to target, you will also need to decide WHERE you want to
target them. As you can see, there are lots of placement options and Meta is constantly
adding new ones. Here are the most important placements to understand…
Make your audiences more targeted by
clicking Exclude or Narrow Audience
Number of people who like baseball, excluding anyone
who also likes cheeseburgers, country music, or cats
AD PLACEMENTS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 57 --

Meta recommends that you advertise everywhere,
but some ads won’t work well with some placements. For
example, basic image ads don’t grab attention on Stories.
Facebook and Instagram Feeds
People are literally addicted to scrolling, and feeds make up the
largest segment of ad inventory. Always leave these on unless
you have an ad specifically designed for another format.
Facebook and Instagram News Stories
Stories are the largest growing segment of ads on Facebook’s
network. They are perfect for anything eye-catching and
engaging.
Facebook Right Column
While these image based ads aren’t the largest source of traffic,
they can perform well, especially when used for retargeting.
Sits and Apps
Meta will also display your ads across a large assortment of web
properties in their network. These ads have never performed well
for us.
NOTE: Not all placement options will be available depending on
the Campaign and Ad type you choose.
While placement is an important part of Ad Set setup, the good
news is Meta will automatically optimize the best places to run your
ads (at least in theory). While it can help to match your placement
to your ad strategy, it is OK to leave as the recommended default if
you’re unsure.
Advertising on Mobile: In addition to the desired platform
placements, there is an additional option to select the devices and
operating systems you want your ads to display. Mobile ads are
generally less expensive and make up the bulk of ad traffic. Desktop
ads are more expensive, but in situations where your landing page
requires more attention (such as long sales videos) it can convert
better. Also, the specific device can sometimes matter. For example,
high tech offers usually do well with an iPhone (iOS) crowd.
30
You can hover over
any placement to
learn more
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 57 --

Realize that there are never-ending bells and whistles
in the Meta Ad manager. Don’t get bogged down. If we
don’t tell you to use it, chances are you can skip it.
OTHER AD SET SETTINGS
Here are the less frequently used settings within the Ad Set level…
Offer
This is a feature that allows you to offer unique deals through Facebook. It can be used
for local promotions or potentially offering coupon codes for ecommerce shops.
More Options (in Placement)
In addition to device type, there are other options for Placement. This includes various
types of exclusions, which while aren’t mission critical, might be relevant if you’re
concerned with brand safety.
Optimization and Cost Control
Some of these settings can be redundant if you’re running a Conversion Objective.
Regarding Cost Control, this can be useful, once you are clear on what to actually
expect. While this can seem like a safe thing to do, be careful about limiting your costs
too much as it can hurt your ability to get ads served.
More Options (in Optimization)
By default, your attribution (meaning what conversions get credited to Facebook) will
be 7 days. We recommend keeping it there. Realize it will never be perfect, which is
just part of the game. Regarding “when you get charged”, this will always be based on
impressions, unless you’re doing a unique Campaign type (such as Video Views).
31
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

The best way to stay organized in your Ads Manager is by using
predetermined naming conventions for your Campaigns, Ad Sets, and Ads.
This will save you a lot of headaches as you grow.
While we provide a recommended naming structure for each level below, there is no set
way to organize your account. You can make your own language and abbreviations as
long as you are consistent.
AD ACCOUNT ORGANIZATION
32
Recommended Naming Structure:
Offer/Product Name - Campaign Objective - Cold/Warm/Hot
Real World Examples:
• Growthcon - Purchase - Warm
• 10XIS - Registration - Cold
• 10X Rule - Reach - Hot
NOTE: We use 10XIS as an abbreviation for 10X Income System.
When you use consistent naming it allows you to search as well
CAMPAIGN LEVEL NAMING
Save Clear

manage, it will also help other people who work on your
ads. Create a standardized naming structure and stick to it.
Recommended Naming Structure:
Audience Type - Audience Details
Real World Examples:
• Page Visitors Last 30 Days - English Speaking
• LLA GrowthCon Buyers - US Only
• All Book Abandoners - Last 7 Days
NOTE: We use LLA as an abbreviation for Lookalike Audience.

-- 34 of 57 --

WHAT IS A PIXEL
PIXEL AND EVENT INSTALLATION
CONVERSIONS, EVENTS
AND AUDIENCES
34
As discussed previously, your pixel is your unique tracking code that tells Meta what
activity happens on your web pages. Note that only people that click on one of your
Meta ads will trigger Meta to send information to the ad manager.
As you may have heard, Apple now gives iPhone users the option to opt-out
of having their activity on websites tracked by 3rd parties like Facebook. In
other words, it stops pixel data from passing back to your ads manager.
While this has made things a little more difficult for small advertisers on
Meta, you shouldn’t let it stop you. While everyone supports privacy,
personalization of ads certainly has its upsides as well, and Meta ads are not
going anywhere.
Note on iOS and its effect on Meta Ads:
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 57 --

INSTALLING YOUR PIXEL
Installing your pixel code should be one of the first steps you take when getting
started. While the exact steps will vary a bit depending on which website or funnel
system you use, the general process is always the same:
STEP 1
Go to the Meta Business Manager
(10Xresource.com/meta), click the hand burger
menu and then Events Manager
STEP 2
Click on Data Sources from the menu on the
left then put an arrow pointing to the Data
Sources image
STEP 3
Click the pixel you want to use (you likely only
have one, so it will be chosen by default)
STEP 4
Click the Add Events dropdown, then From a
New Website
STEP 5
Click the button for Install Code Manually
STEP 6
Copy the provided pixel code
STEP 7
Either install the code yourself, which will vary
based on the type of website you have OR hire
someone to install it for you.
35
On Wordpress, the easiest thing to do is generally to install a plugin, and
then follow their detailed instructions (10xresource.com/fba30).
If you use Clickfunnels, you simply paste the pixel code into the header
field of any funnel or individual page (10xresource.com/fba27)
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 36 of 57 --

STANDARD EVENTS AND SETUP
Install the “pixel helper” to ensure your
pixel is properly installed and working
36
While it might be a good idea to just hire someone to do
this stuff for you, you will always want to double check to
make sure your pixel and events are set up properly. We
highly recommend using the Facebook Pixel Helper Tool
(10xresource.com/fba31). You can easily add it to the
Chrome web browser, then on any page, click it to
confirm everything is working properly.
After you’ve got your pixel installed, there is another step, which is to add specific
conversion Events to the pixel code on the appropriate pages. This is what tells Meta
which of the desired actions a user has taken, allowing Meta to optimize for your
desired Objective.
Here are a few of the most commonly used Standard Events:
One thing to understand is that all of these events function exactly the same. You can
choose any of them as long as it describes what you are trying to achieve.
Add to Cart
Complete Registration
Donate
Initiate Checkout
Lead
Purchase
Schedule
Start Trial
Submit application
Subscribe
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
<!--Meta Pixel Code-->
If you use a different type of site or funnel, you can likely just search their company
documentation for the best way to install.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 57 --

When it comes to tech stuff, I don’t like to waste a minute
of time. If this stuff is over your head, just try going to
Fiverr and searching “install pixel”. Easy peasy.
Then your next step is to have the specific Event name added to your pixel code on the
page that indicates that Event happened. For example, you might add the Lead Event on
the page that comes after a user books an appointment.
Once you’ve got the pixel on your site, and the specific desired
Event added to the appropriate pages, you will start collecting the
data needed to create Custom Audiences and Lookalike
Audiences. You will also be able to start running ad Campaigns
with the Conversion Objective (for example, Purchases!) To
manage your Events and see activity, go to the Events Manager
inside the Meta Business Manager.
Note that every page with your pixel will
trigger a “PageView” event by default
37
ADVANCED NOTE:
If you run many websites, you might want to explore what is called Custom
Conversions. Custom Conversions work very similarly to Standard Events, but
allow you to create your own uniquely named events. This can help keep data
from multiple sites separate.
ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:
• Pixel Installation Documentation (10xresource.com/fba18)
• Standard Event Documentation (10xresource.com/fba19)
• Pixel Helper for Chrome Browser (10xresource.com/fba31)

-- 38 of 57 --

CREATING CUSTOM AUDIENCES
One of the big payoffs for setting up your Pixel and setting up your Events is that you
will be able to create newer, more targeted audiences. The first audience type, and
likely most valuable, are Custom Audiences.
Custom Audiences can be made up of any combination of users who have somehow
interacted with any of the following...
There are certainly more options, but those will be the ones you primarily use.
You need to reach people as many times on as many
channels as possible. Think of your Custom Audiences the
same way as you would your email or text message list.
CUSTOM AUDIENCES AND
RETARGETING
38
• Triggered Events (standard or custom)
• Visited your website (frequency, time)
• Visited particular page (frequency, time)
• Customer list (imported or integrated)
• Page (viewers, followers, clickers)
• Instagram (viewers, followers, clickers)
• Videos (viewers for various times)
• Others including Messenger, etc
Your Website(s) 	Facebook Properties
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 39 of 57 --

CREATING RETARGETING ADS
To get started building Custom Audiences, go to
Audiences from your Business Manager. When you click
on the Create Audience button you’ll notice you can also
create Lookalike Audiences, which we will cover that
next. You’ll also see Saved Audiences, which includes any
audiences you created and saved in the Ad Manager
using standard targeting features.
When you create a Custom Audience you’ll quickly realize
that you have limitless possibilities. Rather than try to
explain every possible combination or strategy, here is a
real world example.
This audience includes people who visited any
webpage which contains the URL
10xincomesystem.com at least 2 times (so for
example, that would include
10xincomesystem.com/all-bonuses,
10xincomesystem.com/what-it-takes, etc)
This audience would exclude anyone who triggered
the Purchase event, which is installed on the thank
you page (the page after the purchase is completed)
Once someone triggers this criteria they will remain
in this audience for 30 days before being removed
39
You can continue to narrow or expand however you’d like.
After you’ve created a Custom Audience, you will now have the ability to run
retargeting ads (sometimes referred to as “remarketing”). It is important to
understand that retargeting ads are functionally no different than normal ads,
they are simply displayed to users who are included in a specific Custom Audience.
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 40 of 57 --

RETARGETING AD STRATEGY
This audience is pulled from Hubspot, our CRM system
40
When you get to the Ad Set step in the ad setup process, you’ll now be able to
search for and select your Custom Audience. Note that the size of your audience
is relevant, and generally Meta won’t display your list size if it is under 1000.
The good news is that you can run ads to any Custom Audience as long as it has
at least 20 or more users.
We cover this in more detail in the Ad Strategy Checklist (be sure to watch the
overview video). You will also see great examples of our retargeting ads inside
the Meta Ad Swipe Files.
You can create retargeting ads for any number of situations. For example, we
run specific ads for people who land on our webinar registration pages, but
don’t actually register. This allows Grant to speak directly to people and get
them to complete registration.
Generally speaking, we find it helps to think of people being different buckets,
which we label based on a temperature scale.
COLD
AUDIENCES
Examples:
Demographics, Interests,
Lookalikes
1 WARM
AUDIENCES
Examples:
Ad Engagers, Ad Clickers,
Page Followers,
Website Visitors
2 	HOT
AUDIENCES
Examples:
Product Buyers,
Cart Abandoners

Select Your Source
This can either be a value-based source (such as a pixel) or from other sources
(such as a Custom Audience or your Facebook Page).
Select Audience Location
Include the countries or geographic areas you want your audience to be located in.
Select Audience Size
You can create multiple audiences at once. The percentages represent how similar
Metax thinks the new Lookalike Audiences are to your original source. 1% are the most
similar.
CREATING LOOKALIKE AUDIENCES
41
Lookalike Audiences are one of the most powerful tools in Meta
advertising. They allow you to take your Custom Audiences, or any
other user data, and create new Audiences that have similarities to
those people. After you set up your Lookalike Audiences, as we’ll
explain here, Meta does the work finding people they believe meet
the correct criteria.
To create Lookalike Audiences, you will again start in Business Manager in the Audience
section. Here are the steps involved…

Once you understand how to create the various audience
types, you can begin to layer them and refine them. Keep
trying stuff, and when you find a winner, drill it deep.
Once you’ve created your Lookalike Audiences, they will appear in the dropdown of the
Custom Audience selection (in the Ad Set setup step).
Lookalike Audiences almost always outperform standard targeting
42
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 43 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Don’t be afraid to be bold in your ads, even a little crazy.
It’s OK if some people don’t like you. What’s not OK is if
nobody notices or remembers you. Get them talking.
CREATING EFFECTIVE
META ADS
CRAFTING HOOKS AND AD COPY
43
If you’ve ever been on Facebook or Instagram, you already know that there are a
LOT of advertisements. How are you going to stand out in the crowd?
To start with the basics, you want to think about your objective in 2 separate steps...
Notice that we did not say “sell a product” or “generate a lead”. Unless you have an
Instagram or Facebook Shop, you usually won’t sell anything on Meta platforms..
That is the job of your funnel or website. One of the common mistakes that
marketers make is trying to do too much. Take things one step at a time.
Always put the most time and attention into what viewers will see when they first
scroll to your ad. What you say on minute 2 of a long video ad doesn’t matter if
they don’t stick around to see it. You’ve got to HOOK them.
1. Grab their attention - your “hook”
2. Get them interested enough to click
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 44 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
When writing your ads or creating your videos, always keep in mind what matters most
to the viewer. While many so-called “gurus” will tell you that you need to use some
deep psychology, the reality is you just need to help people understand how clicking
the button can help them get what they want. Generally speaking, people are
motivated by one of two things...
Regarding the actual copy (text) of your ads, here is a very simple framework
you can use...
1. Immediate Hook
2. Details/Story/Benefits
3. Call to Action
44
Example of a simple,
yet highly effective ad
What made this ad work so well…
• It is bright and attention grabbing
• The ad copy starts by calling out the audience
• The content and headline make the benefit
crystal clear
• Good use of emojis in the copy to keep
people reading
• The ad copy ends with a very clear call to action
• Everybody loves free!
Seek Pleasure OR 	Avoid Pain
(or a combination of both!)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 45 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
VIDEO AND IMAGE AD TIPS
One thing that wasn’t done in the example above was the use of a story.
As long as you’ve provided an effective hook that immediately tells the
viewer “what’s in it for me”, you can go as deep as you want after that.
The more you can create a bond through real-world storytelling, the
better. This could be done in many ways, such as the origin story of why
your product was created, or a customer transformation.
QUICK IMAGE AD TIPS
Colors
Using attention grabbing colors. Anything bright is good. If you’re using conservative
colors, red always stands out.
Faces
People are drawn to people, especially smiling faces. Happy people using your
product is even better.
Add Text
Facebook used to regulate the amount of text an image ad could have, but that is no
longer the case. Convey your value proposition.
Small Logo
There is value in helping people remember your company, but it should not take up
much of the prime real estate.
Dimensions
If your images are wider than they are tall, they won’t take up much scroll space.
You can never go wrong with square.
Examples of ad variations we tested using Facebook’s Dynamic Creative feature
45
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 46 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Don’t think that your ads need to be fancy to convert.
The reality is that an ugly ad with a strong message will
always out-convert a pretty ad with a weak call to action.
QUICK VIDEO AD TIPS
Lighting
Specifically, make sure any faces are well lit.
Audio
The quality of your audio is actually more important than video quality. Beware of the
wind and background noise. Consider buying a lavalier mic.
Movement
Users are more drawn to watch videos with movement than ones that stand still.
Pacing
While this may vary based on your audience, generally speaking energetic and quick
moving videos produce action (think infomercial).
Tone
If you’re mono-toned and boring, people will tune out quickly.
Humor
Nothing beats a little humor for getting people to share and like.
One thing we don’t recommend worrying a lot about at first is buying expensive
equipment. Everything above can be handled with any modern smartphone.
There are countless different design
sizes for each media type on all the
various platforms. We’ve found it
easiest/best to keep design
dimensions as universal as possible.
Here is what we generally use:
Image ads for all: 1350 x 1350 pixels
Instagram videos: 1080 x 1920 pixels
Facebook videos: 1920 x 1080 pixels
RECOMMENDED DESIGN SPECS:
46
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 47 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Assuming you are not a designer, then this might now mean much to you. Your options
are to either hire a designer, use a third-party service like Fiverr (10xresource.com/fiverr)
or use a tool that makes it easy to do it yourself.
Effective Meta advertising can require testing a lot of ideas. To help you with that process,
here are 3 ideas on how to keep it fresh...
If you’ve used Facebook as a user, you’ve probably seen Meta ads as somewhat of a
nuisance. You’ve got to flip your mindset, and start to have the ads be the part of your
news feed that you look for. You can learn from every ad, even the bad ones.
FINDING IDEAS AND INSPIRATION
OBSERVE META ADS DAILY
Facebook does provide basic editing tools.
Easy Image Ad Designer
Use pre-built templates and
customize with your specific
product and message.
Get it at 10xresource.com/canva
Easy Video Ad Creator
Select from a wide variety of
videos and then add your own
text and call to action.
Get it at 10xresource.com/promo
47
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 48 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
Most people think you need to be super creative to be
successful at advertising, but that’s simply not the truth.
Find what works, then change it a bit to be your own.
Pay attention to what grabs you, and when you find something that you like, save it for
later. You can do this by clicking the 	• • • 	in the top right. It is good practice to keep a
saved ads folder of all the best ads you come across.
As a part of their efforts to give user’s more control over their experience, Facebook
allows anyone to see the ads that have been run by any company on their platform. To do
this, simply go to any Facebook Page and go to the “Page Transparency” section, found
on the left side of the screen when you scroll down. Click See All and then Go To Ads
Library and you’re in.
The obvious companies to look at first are your competitors. This will give you an idea
what type of ads they are posting, and also how actively they are advertising. Beyond
competitors, it is highly recommended that you look at successful companies in other
industries. That is where some of the best ideas come from!
USE PAGE TRANSPARENCY
48
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
META AD INSPIRATION
49
To assist advertisers with their creativity, Meta created a tool to help discover
successful ad designs called Ad Inspiration (10xresource.com/fba32) You can quickly
and easily sort through ads in different formats (video, images, etc) as well as ads for
different platforms (FB, Instagram, Messenger, etc).
When you find an ad concept that you like, you can always get a freelance designer to
create them, or use a service like Fiverr (10xresource.com/fiverr).
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 50 of 57 --

TIPS, TRICKS, AND HACKS
As you know, Facebook and Instagram are social platforms. While your goal is likely to
sell something, without participation from the community, it is hard to get traction.
We’ve found that of all the factors involved in ad success, one of the greatest is
engagement from users. It is the ultimate social proof.
While the most common Objective for your ads is Conversion
(leads, purchases, etc) this can often result in very little
engagement, especially as a new advertiser. When you create a
new ad, consider starting it with a different Campaign Objective:
Engagement! Engagement Campaigns will display your ad to
people who are likely to click like and leave a comment.
#1 	The Conversion Booster
3.4K 65 Comments 	3 Likes 1 Comment
Which of these ads are you more likely to click?
50
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 51 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
51
STEP 1
Run your new ad as an Engagement ad first. Only spend a little bit of money, $10 to
$20 is usually sufficient.
STEP 2
After you’ve accumulated some social proof, run the same ad post as a Conversion ad.
Instead of looking like a newbie, you’ll have a little credibility, leading to better
potential results!
This is truly one of the best kept secrets on Facebook!
#2 	Facebook Video Poll Ads
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 52 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
It is likely that every ad you’ve ever seen on Facebook follows the same familiar button
format. We’ve stumbled upon a very different configuration for video ads. As you can
see here, Meta gives you the options to transform videos into highly noticeable and
highly clickable polls.
STEP 1
Create an ad as normal and add a video for the media.
STEP 2
Click to edit the video (note that your page may look different depending on
placement)
STEP 3
Click on Poll under the Creative Tool section.
STEP 4
Write an engaging poll question and provide two options for users to choose
from. Note that each of these has a character limit which is denoted.
52
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 53 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
STEP 5
Add a URL for each of the two poll options. This is where users are sent after clicking,
and they can be the same website if you wish.
STEP 6
Save the poll and then publish your ad as usual.
It is important to note that while this strategy usually leads to higher click rates and
lower click costs, it does give the user a different experience after clicking the poll
buttons. Your video will continue to play, and the web page will be displayed below
the video. For that reason, simple landing pages work best.
This an advanced strategy that is best used once you’ve spent significant money and
have significant data to help guide your decision.
Depending on what you sell, it may be that there are certain times of day when users
are more likely to click, subscribe or purchase. Before implementing this technique,
you should first determine if targeting particular times could be beneficial.
STEP 1
Directly above the columns in your Ads Manager
click the Breakdown button, which looks like this
STEP 2
Click By Delivery and then select Time of Day (Viewer’s Time Zone)
STEP 3
Study the results to see if you notice any noticeable patterns in factors such as Cost
Per Link Click, etc.
If you happen to find a potential breakthrough, then this strategy could have a big
payoff. For example, if you find your CPM (cost per 1000 exposures) drops by 50%
after 5:00, but you know that you do well on sales in the evenings.
Take these steps to implement day parting…
STEP 4
Create a new Campaign, but when you turn on Campaign Budget Optimization
(in the Campaign step) choose Lifetime Budget instead of Daily Budget
53
#3 	Day Parting Ad Strategy
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 54 of 57 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
STEP 5
Click Show More Options and then click edit by Ad Scheduling
STEP 6
Check the box for Run ads on a schedule and then click Next to go to the Ad Set step
STEP 7
When you scroll down to the Budget & Schedule section, you’ll see a calendar like this…
STEP 8`
Click the boxes in the timeslots you want your ads to run. In the example above, ads will
only run from from 5:00 to 9:00 pm on weekdays.
One thing worth noting about this strategy is that since it requires the use of Lifetime
Budget, it does take a little more thought in regard to how much you should be setting
budgets at. Ultimately, will want to incrementally increase or decrease budgets as time
goes on based on your results.
